assessment_items.question_content:
**The blurring of the colors gives an immediate sense of the artist’s process: paint poured onto the canvas when it was wet.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/935f680cbd2dd589ad61069e6b02b995.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This is correct. The blurring of the colors gives an immediate sense of the artist’s process: paint poured onto the canvas when it was wet.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In understanding Abstract Expressionist paintings of the 1950s and 60s, the viewer should focus on . . .**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/935f680cbd2dd589ad61069e6b02b995.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**With many Abstract Expressionist paintings of the 1950s and 60s, it’s important not to get too caught up with possible social and historical contexts and biography, but to focus on what’s before us—the physical elements of the work itself because those elements can tell us so much about the painting.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/American%20Modernism/a/frankenthaler-the-bay)**

choices.content: the physical elements of the work.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Frankenthaler's painting is often characterized as [[☃ dropdown 1]] painting.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/935f680cbd2dd589ad61069e6b02b995.jpg)
hints:
**Frankenthaler's painting is often characterized as Color Field painting. The first generation of Abstract Expressionists, Mark Rothko and Barnett Newman were the first important Color Field painters, while Helen Frankenthaler is often classified as a second-generation member of the group.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/American%20Modernism/a/frankenthaler-the-bay)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The influential mid-20th century art critic, Clement Greenberg, a close friend of Frankenthaler (and Pollock), believed that if art made after the trauma of World War II was going to have any real impact on the social consciousness of Americans it would have to radically change and move towards[[☃ dropdown 1]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/935f680cbd2dd589ad61069e6b02b995.jpg)
hints:
**Clement Greenberg believed that if art made after the trauma of World War II was going to have any real impact on the social consciousness of Americans it would have to radically change and move towards the abstract. For Greenberg, abstraction could reach a more universal and expansive mode of visual communication.**

**[Read this article to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/American%20Modernism/a/frankenthaler-the-bay)**
